clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Evidence-based strategies to improve medication adherence in hypertensive patients
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Poor BP control despite prescribed therapy, patient-reported non-adherence, refill gaps, or suboptimal therapeutic response
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Selection of appropriate adherence-enhancing strategy based on patient context and barriers

navigationPath.purpose: Integrate patient values and shared decision-making

adherenceBarrier.validation: polypharmacy, side effects, forgetfulness, cost, psychosocial, access

currentRegimenComplexity.validation: once daily, twice daily, three or more times daily

validationRules: At least one adherence barrier should be identified
BP should be documented before and after intervention
Patient preference must be recorded for shared decision-making

rules: If pill burden > 1 daily → consider dose consolidation
If multiple separate pills → consider single pill combination
If forgetfulness or routine disruption → offer medication reminders/synchronization
If pharmacist available → refer for education/coaching
If patient has home monitor → activate electronic feedback loop
If stress/anxiety/depression present → offer mindfulness or counseling
Always integrate patient preferences via shared decision-making
Consider self-management interventions for activated patients

categories.indication: Knowledge deficit or need for reinforcement
Multiple medications with different refill dates

crisis.description: false unless BP crisis despite adherence strategies

thresholds.sbp: N/A - adherence strategies are not crisis management

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Implement selected strategy within 1-2 weeks and reassess BP
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Follow up in 4-6 weeks to evaluate adherence improvement
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP at each visit and assess adherence via pill count or pharmacy refill data
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag persistent non-adherence despite multimodal strategies
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log selected adherence strategy and patient preference to care plan
